This follows earlier reports from the IRGC that 25 ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday in coordination with its navy and after receiving Tehran's permission. articles.summary: Strategists at Goldman Sachs increased their year-end target for the US benchmark to 8,000 points, ditching a previous forecast of 7,600. They see earnings growth powered by the AI boom driving further gains in stocks. Michael Msika reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Hormuz Would Reopen One Month After Deal, Iran TV Says
articles.summary: An unofficial draft of a peace deal says the Strait of Hormuz would return to normal within a month, according to Iranian state television. It also says the the US naval blockade would end. Oil prices fell on the news. It's unclear what the US has agreed to. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: Hamas confirms death of newly appointed military chief In a statement delivered on Wednesday, Hamas confirmed the death of Mohammed Odeh, the head of Hamas' armed wing in Gaza, following an Israeli strike. The attack hit a residential building in al-Rimal neighbourhood, west of Gaza City, on Tuesday night. Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said on Wednesday that the Israeli attack had killed Odeh, along with his wife and three children. articles.title: Iran announces reopening of Tabriz airport after bombing
articles.summary: Iran announces reopening of Tabriz airport after bombing Tabriz Shahid Madani International Airport in northwestern Iran is set to reopen for the first time since it was targeted in the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, according to the  International Civil Aviation Organization. “Tabriz airport, which was attacked during the recent war, has now been restored to activity by Iranian specialists and will reopen on Wednesday,” a spokesperson said in an announcement carried in Iranian media. The number of reopened airports has reached 20, according to earlier media reports.
